steps.title: Weigh the study pattern
steps.content: Many studies found a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury in fresh water. One Finnish study did not find that correlation, which led some scientists to wonder whether the association was only a North American pattern. But the passage then says several other studies outside North America, including a study in Sweden, produced results like the North American studies, while few matched the Finnish exception.

steps.title: Draw the broad conclusion
steps.content: Choice A is contradicted by the non-North-American studies that also found the positive association. Choice B discusses absolute levels in Finland, which the passage never compares. Choice C treats Lindstrom's study as flawed, but the passage presents it as supporting the broader pattern. Since most of the evidence points to a positive association across locations, the best conclusion is that the two levels usually move together. The answer is D.
